meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Escape Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. Use our SaaS starter kit, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build revenue-generating products faster.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a revenue killer. Escape it by abandoning generic tutorials for a production-ready SaaS starter kit. By integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation, you shift from passive learning to active shipping. Focus on your business logic, not boilerplate, to accelerate your time-to-market.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks watching YouTube tutorials, stacking up 'To-Do' list clones that will never see a single paying user. You are trapped in the comfort of syntax highlighting, mistaking the act of following instructions for the act of building a business. The market doesn't care about your clean code; it cares about the value you provide. 

True engineering velocity comes from leveraging battle-tested foundations. By adopting a SaaS starter kit, you bypass the soul-crushing boilerplate phase. Armed with Antigravity for structure and Claude Code for execution, you turn your vision into a deployable asset in hours, not months. It is time to stop building toys and start building a company.

steps.title: Define Your MVP Schema
steps.description: Stop guessing your data model. Use Antigravity to define the core entities that drive your business value, ensuring your database schema is optimized for growth from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS platform including user authentication, subscription tiers, and a project management entity. Ensure row-level security is defined.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate database schema based on the Antigravity output and apply migrations'

steps.title: Bootstrap Authentication Flow
steps.description: Auth is a commodity. Do not build it from scratch. Use your starter kit to implement secure, scalable authentication that supports social logins and magic links.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an authentication service configuration using NextAuth and Supabase, including protected routes and user profile synchronization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the auth service provided by Antigravity in the src/lib folder'

steps.title: Automate Stripe Integration
steps.description: Revenue is the only metric that matters. Connect your starter kit to Stripe to handle webhooks, subscription lifecycle, and customer portal management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe webhook handler that updates a user's subscription status in the database when a payment succeeds or fails.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate stripe-webhooks.ts into the API route and verify signature validation'

steps.title: Implement AI-Powered Features
steps.description: Use Claude Code to wrap your core business logic with LLM capabilities, allowing your product to offer unique value that justifies a premium subscription price.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer for OpenAI API integration with error handling, streaming responses, and token usage tracking.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor the existing service layer to include the AI streaming logic from Antigravity'

steps.title: Deploy to Production Infrastructure
steps.description: A product on your local machine is a hobby. A deployed product is a business. Configure your CI/CD pipeline to push to Vercel or Railway with automated testing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel, including build cache optimization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup the .github/workflows directory for deployment'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'feature flags' over 'perfect code' to test market demand before locking in architecture.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests *after* the feature is built to ensure high coverage without slowing down development speed.
Never build your own payment infrastructure; use Stripe's pre-built UI components to save 40+ hours of development time.
Focus on shipping a 'broken' MVP to 10 users rather than a 'perfect' prototype to zero users.
Treat your SaaS starter kit as a living document; update your internal documentation as you modify the boilerplate.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, focus entirely on customer acquisition. Do not touch the codebase for further features until you have 10 paying users or clear qualitative feedback.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the intent. The only variable remaining is your execution. Stop stalling and start shipping the solution your customers are waiting for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
